instruction: Latest APMC Notification
output: An official notification from the Andhra Pradesh Medical Council was issued on May 15th, 2025. Please refer to the linked document for full details.

instruction: Prerequisite for NOC & Good Standing Certificates
output: Applications for No Objection Certificates (NOC) and Good Standing Certificates will only be accepted after the Final Registration Certificate has been issued by the APMC.

instruction: Requirement of Original Certificates for Upload
output: All candidates are required to have all original certificates, from SSC/equivalent up to their highest education (UG/PG), ready for uploading to the server during the application process.

instruction: Application Submission Method
output: Applications for renewal and other services with the A.P Medical Council will not be accepted by Post or through any third-party. Applicants must apply directly.

instruction: Instructions for CME/CPD Programmes
output: Detailed instructions and guidelines for conducting CME/CPD Programmes are available in the document linked below.

instruction: CME Credit Hours for Online Events
output: In accordance with new NMC guidelines, 2 CME Credit Hours/Points will be awarded per day for Online CMEs, Webinars, or Conferences that have a duration of 8 hours.

instruction: Mandatory Slot Booking
output: Slot booking is mandatory for all registration services. Please ensure you are present at least 20 minutes before your scheduled slot time.

instruction: Slot Release Schedule
output: New slots for registration services will be released every Friday at 12:00 Noon.

instruction: Accepted Payment Methods
output: All forms of payment including UPI, Debit Cards, and Credit Cards are accepted for services.

instruction: Certificate Delivery Method
output: Please note that all physical certificates issued by the council will be sent to the applicant's address via Registered Post only.

instruction: NOC Requirement for Post Graduates from Other States
output: All 'Post Graduates' who have studied or worked in another state must first register with that state's Medical Council and obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) before they can register with the A.P Medical Council.

instruction: NOC Issuance Procedure
output: The No Objection Certificate (NOC) will be sent to the concerned State Medical Council and the candidate via Email. No physical copy will be issued.

instruction: Effect of NOC Issuance on Registration Status
output: Once a No Objection Certificate (NOC) is issued, the applicant's registration number will immediately become 'Inactive' in the Andhra Pradesh Medical Register.

instruction: Role and Objective of APMC
output: The Andhra Pradesh Medical Council is a Statutory Body constituted to regulate the practice of modern scientific medicine in Andhra Pradesh, ensuring doctors meet prescribed standards to protect public health and safety.

instruction: Inspection and Enforcement Powers of APMC
output: Section-20 (iv) of the act empowers the Council to visit or inspect any hospital, nursing home, or institution to investigate alleged unethical practices, quackery, or unauthorized practice of modern medicine.

instruction: Details of Medical colleges in Andhra Pradesh
output: A list of recognized medical colleges in the state of Andhra Pradesh is available for download at the provided link.

instruction: Medical Councils in India: Roles and Jurisdictions
output: This document outlines the different Medical Councils functioning in India, detailing their respective roles and jurisdictions.

instruction: Governing Acts and Regulations of APMC
output: The main acts and regulations that govern medical practice and ethics under the Andhra Pradesh Medical Council are: • NMC Act 2019 • NMC/IMC Regulations 2002 • APMPR Act 1986 • APMPR Act 1968 • APMC Rules 1978 • APMPR Amendment Act 2013 • Rules G.O NO.129 Dated 08-08-2013 • APMPR Amendment Act 2018 • IMC Act 1956 • IMC Act Amendments-1 • I.C.M.R Guidelines • Pandemic Act 1857 • Surrogacy Act.

instruction: NMC Act 2019
output: The National Medical Commission Act, 2019, is a primary act governing medical practice and education. The full text is available via the link.

instruction: NMC/IMC Regulations 2002
output: These regulations set ethical guidelines and standards for medical professionals, ensuring safe, ethical, and professional practice in India as defined by the National Medical Commission (NMC) and Indian Medical Council (IMC).

instruction: APMPR Act 1986
output: This is an amendment to the Andhra Pradesh Medical Practitioners Registration Act, governing how medical practitioners are registered and regulating their legal recognition in Andhra Pradesh.

instruction: APMPR Act 1968
output: The main act for consolidating and updating laws about registration of modern medicine practitioners; it covers council formation, registration procedures, and powers regarding medical practitioners in the state.

instruction: APMC Rules 1978
output: Detailed procedural rules made under the APMPR Act guiding the working, registration, inspection, and regulatory mechanisms for practitioners under APMC.

instruction: APMPR Amendment Act 2013
output: An act to update and improve provisions of the original 1968 Act, such as including new categories of practitioners or updating CME (Continuing Medical Education) requirements.

instruction: Rules G.O NO.129 Dated 08-08-2013
output: A government order amending specific rules for state and subordinate services, affecting appointment, regulation, and administration under the medical council.

instruction: APMPR Amendment Act 2018
output: This amendment further revises the registration and regulatory provisions to keep them up to date with current medical practice needs.

instruction: IMC Act 1956
output: The Indian Medical Council Act, the national law for medical education, registration, recognition of qualifications, and the ethical code, establishing uniform standards for medical practice in India.

instruction: IMC Act Amendments
output: Amendments made to IMC Act 1956 for addressing evolving issues in regulation, education, and ethical standards for medical professionals in India.

instruction: I.C.M.R Guidelines
output: Guidelines issued by the Indian Council of Medical Research prescribing ethical standards, research conduct, and professional responsibilities, especially for clinical trials and patient welfare.

instruction: Pandemic Act 1857
output: This old act prescribes preventive and regulatory actions during epidemics and pandemics, providing the government legal powers to control the spread of infectious diseases.

instruction: Surrogacy Act
output: This act regulates and governs surrogacy procedures, defining who can be a surrogate, eligibility for intending parents, and ethical/professional conduct for doctors involved in surrogacy.

instruction: RTI Act Information
output: Information about the RTI (Right to Information) Act and its application within APMC services. The detailed compendium covers powers and functions under the RTI framework.

instruction: Vision of Andhra Pradesh Medical Council(APMC)
output: Our visions forms the basis on which our Council operates. We want every decision we make, every interaction we have, every order we pass and every email we write to embody our values. Each of our actions demonstrates our character, dedication, transparency and beliefs as an organisation. It is important that each members of our organisation acts in a way that is consistent with our values and in a way that reflects the principles we believe in. Protect the public through the work that we do. Treat everyone fairly. Be honest and strive to be open and transparent. Committed to excellence in everything we do. Be a listening and learning organisation

instruction: Mission of Andhra Pradesh Medical Council(APMC)
output: We protect, promote and maintain the health and safety of the public by making sure that Registered Medical Practitioners follow proper standards of medical practice. Maintaining an up-to-date database of qualified doctors possessing recognised degrees in modern scientific medicine within the state of Andhra Pradesh. Fostering good medical practices. Promoting high standards in medical education and training. Dealing firmly and fairly with doctors whose fitness to practice is in doubt. Maintain a Code of Conduct for the Medical Practitioners Registered with this council. Responsible for regular upgrading of knowledge, techniques of practice and to impart the same to the needy in society

instruction: History of Andhra Pradesh Medical Council(APMC)
output: Andhra Pradesh Medical Council is a Body corporate established by an Act of the State Legislature, vide Act No.23 of 1968, by integrating the hitherto Two State Medical Councils, i.e. Hyderabad State Medical Council and Andhra Medical Council. The Council was first constituted by a notification issued in G.O.Ms.No.662 on 19th December, 1991. The Council had been constituted from time to time as per the Act. On earlier occasions, Interim Medical Councils were constituted on 2.1.2012, vide G.O.Rt.No.08 HM & FW (E1) Department and on 28.12.2012, vide G.O.Rt. No.1839, HM & FW Department. The Council is having an Executive Committee constituted under Section-11 of the Act with Chairman, Vice Chairperson of the Council as ex-officio and three other members. There are regularly constituted Committees under Section-12 of the Act, such as Ethics Committee, Administrative Committee, Finance Committee, CME Committee and others.

instruction: Types of Notifications and Alerts for Doctors and Medical Institutions
output: Notification on Generic Drug, 2021 Notifications, 2020 Notifications, MCI Notification regarding Eligibility Certificate, 2017 Notifications, APMC ALERT, APMC NOTICE AND ALERT, Notification on Renewal of Registration - Feb 2017, Information for Medical Graduates, Post Graduates and Institutions.

instruction: Certificate of Good Standing Application Form
output: Application form for obtaining a Certificate of Good Standing for medical practitioners. This certificate is essential for doctors planning to practice abroad or for various professional requirements.

instruction: Temporary Permission Application for Foreign/Outside State Visiting Doctors
output: Application form for temporary permission required by foreign doctors or doctors from outside Andhra Pradesh state who wish to visit and practice temporarily within the state jurisdiction.

instruction: Online services that APMC provide
output: APMC provides the following online services: 1. Medical Registration (Provisional and Permanent) 2. Renewal of Registration 3. Additional Qualification Registration 4. Issuance of Good Standing Certificate 5. Issuance of No Objection Certificate 6. Continuing Medical Education (CME) Credits Management 7. Temporary Registration for Foreign Doctors 8. District-Level Registration and Renewal Services 9. Doctor Profile Management via e-Doctor Portal 10. Inspection and Accreditation Services

instruction: Eligibility for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Andhra Pradesh.
output: Completed M.B.B.S in Andhra Pradesh, Successfully finished Internship, Upon successful verification of profile, login credentials for eDoctor will be sent to registered mobile number

instruction: Procedure or process for Final Registration (PR).
output: Procedure or process for Final Registration 1. Filled in Online Registration Form. 2. Payment for Id Card. 3. Upload Enclosures. 4. Slot Booking. 5. Check Details (Know your Acknowledgement Id, Status & Slot Id). 6. Id Card will be sent through the registered post along with the final registration certificate.

instruction: Procedure or process for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Andhra Pradesh.
output: Fill Online Registration Form (includes Provisional Registration Details, Personal Details, Address, Educational Details & Internship Details), Payment for ID Card, Upload Enclosures: Passport Size Photo (100KB max, jpg/png), SSC, APMC Provisional Registration (TR), Internship certificate, Ethics Awareness, M.B.B.S Degree (200KB max, jpg/png), Notarised Affidavit & Testimonials if necessary (pdf only), Book Slot for Verification, Report in Person at APMC with all Required Documents (Original & Xerox) for Permanent Registration & Allotment of Login Credentials, ID Card will be sent through registered post along with final registration certificate

instruction: Additional information for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Andhra Pradesh.
output: Affidavit (PROFORMA-A) in Original (In case of delay beyond 3 months after completion of internship). Two Testimonials in Original from the concerned Medical College along with Affidavit(PROFORMA-A) (In case of delay beyond 6 months after completion of internship). File name should not contain special characters and should be less than 20 characters. Please report in person at APMC with original documents. ID card and certificate sent via registered post

instruction: What documents are required for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Andhra Pradesh?
output: Filled-in Application Form Original, Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png), SSC / CBSE Certificate (for DOB), APMC Provisional Registration (TR), Internship Certificate, Ethics Awareness Certificate, M.B.B.S Degree, Testimonials if necessary (Notarised Affidavit & Testimonials)

instruction: Eligibility for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State.
output: Eligibility for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State:  Those who have pursed M.B.B.S in Other State and registered in Concerned State Medical Council are eligible to register the Final Medical Registration (Permanent Registration) in APMC.  The Applicants have to submit the No Objection Certificate which was issued by the concerned State Medical Council.  After successful verification of your profile in APMC, login credentials for eDoctor (utilizing the online services of APMC) will be sent to your registered Mobile Number.

instruction: Procedure or process for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State.
output: Procedure for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State:  Filled in Online Registration Form (Includes M.B.B.S Registration Details, Personal Details, Address for Communication, Educational Details, Internship Details & NOC Issued Details).  Payment for Id Card (Id Card will be sent through the registered post along with your registration certificate, after verification of your profile).  Upload Enclosures. 1. Upload Passport Size Photo & Academic Certificates (SSC, Internship, M.B.B.S Degree, Concerned State Registration Certificate, Latest NOC, (Custodian Certificate - if necessary)). 2.Size of the documents to be uploaded. ▪  Photo - 100KB, Other Documents - 200KB. ▪  Documents are allowed jpg / png only, with Proper Naming Conventions. File name should not contain any special characters and should have less than 20 character length.  Slot Booking. 1.   Book the Slot for Verification. 2.   Please Report in Person at APMC with all Required Documents both (Original & Xerox) for the Permanent Registration & Allotment of Login Credentials for eDoctor.  Check Details (Know your Acknowledgement Id, Status & Slot Id).

instruction: Additional information for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State.
output: File name should not contain special characters and should be less than 20 characters. NOC must be from the concerned State Medical Council. Please report in person at APMC with original documents

instruction: What documents are required for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other State?
output: Filled-in original application form, Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png), SSC / CBSE Certificate (for DOB), Internship Certificate, M.B.B.S Degree, Concerned State Registration Certificate, Latest NOC from State Medical Council, Custodian Certificate (if necessary)

instruction: Eligibility for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other Country.
output: Eligibility for Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other Country: FMGs who have completed their M.B.B.S degree abroad and passed the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE) are eligible.  FMGs must complete a mandatory 1 to 3 years internship, based on compensation for online study during the COVID-19 pandemic as per National Medical Commission (NMC) guidelines.  Verification of the medical degree's authenticity by the university and Indian Embassy is required before registration.  Permanent Registration is granted only after successful document verification and completion of the prescribed internship.

instruction: Procedure or process For Final Medical Registration for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other Country (FMGs).
output: Procedure for Final Registration Filled in Online Registration Form. Payment for Id Card.  Upload Enclosures.  Slot Booking.  Check Details (Know your Acknowledgement Id, Status & Slot Id).  Id Card will be sent through the registered post along with the final registration certificate

instruction: Additional information for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other Country (FMGs).
output: Internship duration is 1 to 3 years based on COVID-19 online study compensation. Medical degree must be verified by both the university and the Indian Embassy. Internship duration is as per National Medical Commission guidelines

instruction: What documents are required for M.B.B.S Graduates in Other Country (FMGs)?
output: Passport Size Photo, FMGE Pass Certificate, M.B.B.S Degree (verified by university and Indian Embassy), Internship Completion Certificate, Authenticity verification documents from university, Indian Embassy verification documents

instruction: Eligibility for Profile Registration (Signup):
output: Eligibility for Profile Registration (Signup): Those who have registered in APMC and obtained APMC Registration Number before 25 May, 2023 are eligible for Signup.  Those who have already verified your profile in APMC by Capturing of Digital Information like (Photo, Thumb & Signature) need not to apply again.  After successful verification of your profile, login credentials for eDoctor (utilizing the online services of APMC) will be sent to your registered Mobile Number.

instruction: Procedure or process for Profile Registration (Signup) OR For Doctor Signup.
output: Procedure for Profile Registration (Signup), OR For Doctor Signup: Filled in Profile Registration Form (Includes Personal Details, Address for Communication, Educational Details).  Payment for Id Card (Id Card will be sent through the registered post, after verification of your profile).  Upload Enclosures. 1.   Upload Passport Size Photo & Academic Certificates (SSC, Intermediate, Internship, M.B.B.S Degree, Final Medical Registration (PR), etc,.). 2.   Those who obtained Additional Qualifications (P.G) upload Additional Qualification Registration Certificate only, which was issued by the APMC. Don't upload Provisional / OD. 3.   Size of the documents to be uploaded. ▪  Photo - 100KB, Other Documents - 200KB. ▪  Documents are allowed jpg / png only, with Proper Naming Conventions. File name should not contain any special characters and should have less than 20 character length.  Slot Booking. 1.   Book the Slot for Verification. 2.   Please Report in Person at APMC with all Original (UG / PG) Certificates for the Allotment of Login Credentials of eDoctor.  Check Details (Know your Acknowledgement Id, Status & Slot Id).

instruction: Additional information for Profile Registration (Signup).
output: File name should not contain special characters and should be less than 20 characters. Only for registrations obtained before 25 May 2023. For PG qualifications, upload only AQR Certificate from APMC. Report with original UG/PG certificates

instruction: What documents are required for Profile Registration (Signup)?
output: Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png), SSC Certificate, Intermediate Certificate, Internship Certificate, M.B.B.S Degree, Final Medical Registration (PR) Certificate, Additional Qualification Registration Certificate (if applicable)

instruction: Eligibility Online for eDoctor or how to Apply Services Through eDoctor.
output: Eligibility Online for eDoctor or how to Apply Services Through eDoctor: Those who have registered in APMC and verified your profile by Capturing of Digital Photo, Thumb & Signature are eligible for eDoctor Login.  The eDoctor login credentials have been sent to your registered mobile number via SMS.  while facing any issues in login, Please feel free to contact apmc technical team 0866 245 5297 (only on working days from 10:30 AM - 05:30 PM).

instruction: Procedure or process To Apply Services Through Online for eDoctor Or For Doctor registration/signup.
output: Procedure or process To Apply Services Through Online for eDoctor Or For Doctor registration/signup. Home (Includes Profile, UG & PG Details, Certificates etc,).  e-Services 1.   Apply for required Service's (Good Standing, NOC, Renewal & Duplicate). 2.   Upload required documents based on service. 3.   Size of the documents to be uploaded. ▪  Photo - 100KB, Other Documents - 200KB. ▪  Documents are allowed jpg / png only, with Proper Naming Conventions. File name should not contain any special characters and should have less than 20 character length. 4.   Payment for applied service's based on application type (Tatkal / Non-Tatkal).  Subject to Verification from APMC. 1.   If any corrections occured in the application, APMC will reject the application. 2.   The Reject Reason will be sent through an SMS to the applicant for their registered mobile number.  Re Apply 1.   The Rejected application's are available in the Reapply option. 2.   The Rejected Application must be Reapply by the applicant with suggested corrections.  Certificate Generation. 1.   After the final approval certificate will be sent through a registered post.  Services like NOC & Good Standing, Certificate will be sent through an email to the person & an applied council.

instruction: how to login/signin as eDoctor
output: Steps to login as eDoctor (Enter Username, Captcha & OTP and click login button).

instruction: Eligibility for Additional Qualification Registration.
output: Must be registered with APMC. Completed additional medical qualifications (PG degree/diploma). Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials

instruction: Procedure for Additional Qualification Registration.
output: Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement)

instruction: What documents are required for Additional Qualification Registration?
output: Filled-in Application Form Original. Final Registration Certificate & Copy. Diploma / PG / Super Specialty Original and Copy. Renewal of Registration Copies (If any). SSC /CBSE Certificate (for DOB). PG Study Certificate (Bonafide / Conduct). Course Recognition Letter issued by the NMC. Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). Supporting documents as required

instruction: Additional information for Additional Qualification Registration.
output: Candidates with AP Medical Council Registration coming from other states in India shall submit a copy of registration and a valid NOC from the concerned State Medical Council. Those who settled abroad or practicing abroad shall submit copy of recent Visa entry along with a color Xerox copy of the Passport. Tatkal or Non-Tatkal service available. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option. Photo size must be 100KB and other documents must be 200KB in jpg/png format only

instruction: Eligibility for No Objection Certificate (NOC).
output: Must be registered with APMC. Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials. Valid reason for requiring NOC

instruction: Procedure for No Objection Certificate (NOC).
output:
Manual Procedure: Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement).
Online Procedure: Login to eDoctor portal. Navigate to e-Services section. Apply for NOC. Upload required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement). Subject to verification from APMC. If corrections are required, APMC will reject with reason via SMS. Re-apply with corrections if rejected. Certificate will be sent through email to applicant and applied council

instruction: What documents are required for No Objection Certificate (NOC)?
output: Current Registration Certificate. Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). Final Registration Certificate Copy. Renewal of Registration Copies (if any). Letter denoting the purpose for NOC. PG Registration Certificates (if any)

instruction: Additional information for No Objection Certificate (NOC).
output: Tatkal or Non-Tatkal service available. Certificate will be sent via email to the applicant and the applied council. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option

instruction: Eligibility for Good Standing Certificate.
output: Must be registered with APMC. Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials. No disciplinary proceedings pending

instruction: Procedure for Good Standing Certificate.
output:
Manual Procedure: Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement).
Online Procedure: Login to eDoctor portal. Navigate to e-Services section. Apply for Good Standing Certificate. Upload required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement). Subject to verification from APMC. If corrections are required, APMC will reject with reason via SMS. Re-apply with corrections if rejected. Certificate will be sent through email to applicant and applied council

instruction: What documents are required for Good Standing Certificate?
output: Current Registration Certificate. Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). Final Registration Certificate Copy. PG Registration Certificate Copies (if any). Renewal of Registration Copies (If any)

instruction: Additional information for Good Standing Certificate.
output: The candidate shall possess valid registration on the date of application. Tatkal or Non-Tatkal service available. Certificate sent via email to the applicant and the applied council. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option

instruction: Eligibility for Renewal of Medical Registration.
output: Must be registered with APMC. Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials. Registration due for renewal as per APMC guidelines

instruction: Procedure for Renewal of Medical Registration.
output:
Manual Procedure: Book a slot to renew medical registration. Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement).
Online Procedure: Login to eDoctor portal. Navigate to e-Services section. Apply for Renewal. Upload required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement). Subject to verification from APMC. If corrections are required, APMC will reject with reason via SMS. Re-apply with corrections if rejected. Certificate will be sent through registered post after approval

instruction: What documents are required for Renewal of Medical Registration?
output: Current/Expired Registration Certificate. Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). CME Credits proof (if applicable). Supporting documents as required

instruction: Additional information for Renewal of Medical Registration.
output: Delay beyond one year from the date of expiry of registration, no renewal shall be permitted. In such cases, Re-Registration is to be taken. Check APMC guidelines for renewal timeline. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option

instruction: Eligibility for Re-Registration.
output: Previously registered with APMC. Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials. Valid reason for re-registration

instruction: Procedure for Re-Registration.
output:
Manual Procedure: Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement).
Online Procedure: Login to eDoctor portal. Navigate to e-Services section. Apply for Re-Registration. Upload required documents. Make payment (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal as per requirement). Subject to verification from APMC. If corrections are required, APMC will reject with reason via SMS. Re-apply with corrections if rejected. Certificate will be sent through registered post after approval

instruction: What documents are required for Re-Registration?
output: Previous Registration Certificate. Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). Reason or supporting documents for re-registration. All educational certificates

instruction: Additional information for Re-Registration.
output: Tatkal or Non-Tatkal service available. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option

instruction: Eligibility for Duplicate Registration Certificate.
output: Must be registered with APMC. Have verified profile with eDoctor login credentials. Valid reason for duplicate certificate (lost or damaged original)

instruction: Procedure for Duplicate Registration Certificate.
output: Visit APMC office in person. Filled-in Application Form Original. Submit required documents. Make payment (Tatkal or Non-Tatkal as per requirement)

instruction: What documents are required for Duplicate Registration Certificate?
output: Passport Size Photo (100KB, jpg/png). FIR copy (if certificate lost). Affidavit (if certificate lost or damaged). Damaged certificate (if applicable). Final Registration Certificate Copy. Police Report (FIR). Affidavit Original (PROFORMA-B)

instruction: Additional information for Duplicate Registration Certificate.
output: In the event of finding the original registration certificate by any chance, the duplicate certificate shall be surrendered to the APMC office. Tatkal or Non-Tatkal service available. FIR and affidavit are required if the certificate is lost. Rejected applications are available in Re-apply option

instruction: Who is the registrar of APMC?
output: The registrar of APMC is DR Idimadakala Ramesh.

instruction: Can foreign doctors register online at APMC?
output: Yes, APMC provides temporary registration services for foreign doctors who fulfill eligibility criteria.

instruction: Does APMC offer CME credit management as an online service?
output: Yes, CME credits management is available as part of APMC's online services.

instruction: Is Doctor Profile Management available through the eDoctor portal?
output: Yes, doctors can manage their profile, UG/PG details, and certificates through eDoctor login.

instruction: Can I renew my medical registration online?
output: Yes, APMC allows online renewal through the eDoctor portal.

instruction: Who is eligible for final medical registration in Andhra Pradesh?
output: MBBS graduates who completed their degree and internship in Andhra Pradesh are eligible for permanent registration.

instruction: What happens after successful profile verification for AP graduates?
output: Login credentials for the eDoctor portal are sent to the registered mobile number of the applicant.

instruction: Where can I apply for final medical registration?
output: You can apply for final medical registration at the official APMC portal.

instruction: What documents should be uploaded for final registration?
output: Photo (100KB), SSC, Provisional Registration, Internship, Ethics Awareness, MBBS Degree, notarized affidavit/testimonials (if needed).

instruction: What are the allowed file formats and sizes for upload?
output: Photo - JPG/PNG up to 100KB; other documents - JPG/PNG up to 200KB; Testimonials - PDF only.

instruction: Are there naming guidelines for uploaded files?
output: Yes, file names must not contain special characters and should be under 20 characters.

instruction: Do I need to report in person after slot booking?
output: Yes, applicants must visit APMC with original and Xerox copies for verification and credential allotment.

instruction: Who is eligible for final registration from other states?
output: MBBS graduates registered in another state medical council and holding a valid NOC from that council.

instruction: Is submission of NOC mandatory for other state graduates?
output: Yes, applicants must upload the No Objection Certificate issued by their state medical council.

instruction: Which documents must be uploaded for other state graduates?
output: Passport photo, SSC, Internship certificate, MBBS degree, State Registration Certificate, Latest NOC, and Custodian Certificate (if necessary).

instruction: What is the application link for other state graduates?
output: Other state graduates can apply for final registration using the APFMR portal.

instruction: Are file size requirements the same for other state graduates?
output: Yes, photos (100KB) and other documents (200KB) in JPG/PNG; naming conventions apply.

instruction: Do other state graduates need to report in person?
output: Yes, all applicants must appear at APMC for original document verification.

instruction: Who can apply for final registration as foreign medical graduates (FMGs)?
output: FMGs who completed MBBS abroad and passed the FMGE exam are eligible.

instruction: Is internship mandatory for FMGs?
output: Yes, a 1-3 year internship is required per NMC guidelines.

instruction: Does APMC verify medical degrees obtained abroad?
output: Yes, degrees must be verified by both the university and the Indian Embassy.

instruction: When is permanent registration granted to FMGs?
output: After successful document verification and completion of internship.

instruction: What is the FMG Doctor registration link?
output: Foreign Medical Graduates can apply for registration using the OCFMR portal.

instruction: Who is eligible for APMC profile registration or signup?
output: Doctors registered before May 25, 2023, with a valid APMC registration number.

instruction: Should I reapply if I already captured photo/thumb/signature at APMC?
output: No, reapplication is not required.

instruction: When will I receive login credentials for profile registration?
output: After successful profile verification, credentials are sent to your registered mobile number.

instruction: Which documents must be uploaded for profile registration?
output: Passport photo, SSC, Intermediate, Internship, MBBS degree, and Final Registration certificate.

instruction: Which document is required for PG-qualified doctors for profile registration?
output: Only the Additional Qualification Registration Certificate issued by APMC; do not upload provisional or original degree copies.

instruction: What are the file restrictions?
output: Photo (100KB) and other documents (200KB), file names under 20 characters, allowed JPG/PNG formats.

instruction: Must doctors appear in person after slot booking?
output: Yes, bring all original certificates for verification and eDoctor login credential allotment.

instruction: Who is eligible for the eDoctor login?
output: Doctors registered in APMC and verified with digital photo, thumb, and signature.

instruction: How do I get the login credentials for eDoctor?
output: Credentials are sent to the registered mobile number via SMS after successful verification.

instruction: What should I do if I face login issues?
output: Contact the APMC Technical Team at 0866-2455297 (Monday-Friday, 10:30 AM-5:30 PM).

instruction: What happens if my application is rejected?
output: You will receive an SMS with the rejection reason. You can correct and reapply under the Reapply option.

instruction: How are certificates delivered?
output: After approval, certificates are sent by registered post. For NOC or Good Standing, they are emailed to the applicant and the council.

instruction: What is the eDoctor login URL?
output: The login URL for the eDoctor portal is available at the provided link.

instruction: Can all APMC services be accessed through eDoctor?
output: Yes, eDoctor enables online application, payment, and tracking for all major APMC services.

instruction: Is payment mandatory before verification?
output: Yes, ID card and service fee payments must be done during application submission.

instruction: Can the general public access APMC registration details online?
output: No, only registered doctors with eDoctor login can access individual details securely.

instruction: Does APMC provide public verification of registered doctors?
output: Yes, the website allows public search of registered medical practitioners under APMC jurisdiction.

instruction: Does APMC send certificates physically or digitally?
output: Certificates are sent via registered post; certain services (NOC, Good Standing) are emailed.

instruction: What is the purpose of checking slot availability on the APMC website?
output: The purpose of checking slot availability is to know the available time slots for doctor registration or document submission before booking an appointment online.

instruction: What is the official website link to check slot availability?
output: You can  check slot availability at the official APMC portal.

instruction: Can I check availability for future dates?
output: Yes, you can select a future date from the calendar and check if slots are available for that day.

instruction: What happens if no slots are available for the selected date?
output: If no slots are available, you can choose another date or slot type (Tatkal/Non-Tatkal) to find an open time.

instruction: Is login required to check slot availability?
output: No, you can check slot availability directly without logging in.

instruction: What should I do after checking slot availability?
output: Once you find an available slot, proceed to the Slot Booking section on the APMC website to confirm your appointment.

instruction: Can I modify my selected slot after checking availability?
output: No, checking slot availability only displays open slots. You can select or modify your slot during the booking process.

instruction: Why is it important to check slot availability before booking?
output: Checking slot availability helps ensure that your preferred date and slot type are open before you proceed with booking, avoiding any scheduling conflicts or delays.

instruction: What is the purpose of slot booking on the APMC website?
output: Slot booking allows doctors to reserve a specific date and time to visit the Andhra Pradesh Medical Council (APMC) office for verification, document submission, or registration services.

instruction: What is the official website link for slot booking?
output: You can book your slot online at the official APMC doctor slot booking portal.

instruction: What is the difference between Tatkal and Non-Tatkal slot booking?
output:
1. Tatkal Slots: For urgent or fast-track processing (4 working days turnaround). 
2. Non-Tatkal Slots: For normal processing (3 weeks turnaround).

instruction: Why is mobile number verification required during slot?
output: Mobile verification ensures that appointment details and notifications are sent to the correct doctor's registered mobile number for confirmation and communication.

instruction: Can I change my slot date or time after booking?
output: No, once the slot is booked, it cannot be modified. You must cancel and rebook a new slot if changes are needed.

instruction: What happens after a slot is successfully booked?
output: You will receive a confirmation message on your verified mobile number. You must visit the APMC office on the booked date and time with all required documents.

instruction: Is there a limit on how many slots can be booked per day?
output: Yes, only a limited number of slots are available per day to manage doctor verification efficiently. Early booking is recommended.

instruction: What should I do if I don't receive a confirmation message after booking?
output: Check your mobile number entry and SMS inbox. If not received, recheck the booking status on the website or contact the APMC helpdesk.

instruction: Can I book slots on holidays or weekends?
output: No, slot booking is available only for working days (Monday to Friday) during official working hours.

instruction: What is the official address of the Andhra Pradesh Medical Council (APMC)?
output:
The APMC office is located at: 
2nd Floor, Dr. N.T.R. University of Health Sciences, Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh-520008

instruction: How can I contact the APMC office for inquiries?
output:
You can reach APMC through the following channels: 
• Phone: 0866 245 5280 / 0866 245 5281 
• WhatsApp: 90309 99616

instruction: What are the counter service timings at APMC?
output: Counter services are available from 10:00 AM to 04:30 PM on working days.

instruction: When is the lunch break at the APMC office?
output: The lunch break is from 01:30 PM to 02:00 PM.

instruction: What are the working days of APMC?
output:
APMC operates from Monday to Friday. 
The office remains closed on Saturdays, Sundays, and public holidays.

instruction: What is the Service Turnaround Time (TAT) for APMC services?
output:
1. Tatkal Services: 4 Working Days per service 
2. Non-Tatkal Services: 3 Weeks per service

instruction: What does “Tatkal” mean in APMC services?
output: “Tatkal” refers to urgent or priority processing, where the requested service is completed within 4 working days.

instruction: What does “Non-Tatkal” mean in APMC services?
output: “Non-Tatkal” refers to regular processing, where the service is completed within 3 weeks.

instruction: Can I visit APMC without an appointment during counter hours?
output: It is recommended to book a slot online before visiting the APMC office to ensure faster service and avoid waiting time.

instruction: How can I get updates or assistance from APMC?
output: You can contact the APMC office through phone or WhatsApp for queries related to registration, document verification, or application status.

instruction: What is the purpose of the Medical Registration Search?
output: The Medical Registration Search allows users to verify a doctor's registration details with the Medical Council to ensure they are legally permitted to practice medicine.

instruction: What details are required to search by Doctor name?
output:
To search by name, you need to enter: 
• Doctor's Name (Mandatory) 
• Gender (Mandatory) 
• Father's Name 
Then click on “Search by Name.”

instruction: What details are required to search Doctor by registration number?
output: You need to enter the Final Medical Registration Number and then click on “Search by Registration No.”

instruction: Where can I access the Medical Registration Search option?
output: You can access it on the official Medical Council website by clicking the link provided under the “Medical Registration Search” section.

instruction: What information will I get after searching for a doctor?
output: The search results display the doctor's Name, Registration Number, Qualification, Date of Registration, and Medical Council Name.

instruction: Can I search for a Doctor using partial details?
output: Yes, you can enter partial details (like only the first name) to get a list of matching records, but providing complete information ensures accurate results.

instruction: What should I do if no Doctor record is found?
output: Check for spelling errors or incorrect registration numbers and try again. If the issue persists, contact the Medical Council for assistance.

instruction: Is the Medical Registration Search available for all states?
output: No, it is specific to the respective State Medical Council (e.g., Andhra Pradesh Medical Council). Each state has its own database.

instruction: Why is Medical Registration Search important?
output: It helps verify the authenticity of medical practitioners and ensures that only qualified and registered doctors are practicing legally.

instruction: What is the official link for CME/CPD registration?
output: The CME/CPD registration can be done at https://online.apmc.ap.gov.in/cmeregistration.

instruction: Who can register for CME/CPD programmes in APMC?
output: Any authorized medical organization, institution, hospital, or educational body eligible under APMC guidelines can register.

instruction: What is CME or CPD in the context of APMC?
output: CME means Continuing Medical Education and CPD means Continuing Professional Development     both are approved academic programmes to upgrade doctors' skills.

instruction: What documents are required for CME organization registration?
output: Organization Registration Certificate, Address proof, Contact details, and other official details in PDF format (under 400KB).

instruction: Is there a file size limit for CME registration documents?
output: Yes, each uploaded document must be under 400KB.

instruction: Can multiple organizations register under the same account?
output: No, each organization must register with a unique user ID and acknowledgment ID.

instruction: Will I receive confirmation after registration?
output: Yes, a provisional approval and acknowledgment ID are generated after successful submission.

instruction: Why can a CME/CPD programme application get rejected?
output: Rejection may occur due to incomplete documentation, late submission, incorrect information, or failure to follow APMC guidelines.

instruction: Are rejected CME applications automatically deleted?
output: No, they remain accessible under the Re-Apply section for correction and resubmission.

instruction: Will the same acknowledgment ID be used for re-application?
output: Yes, you can use the same previously generated acknowledgment ID to reapply.

instruction: What can organizations manage through their CME dashboard?
output: Program applications, approval status, event details, document uploads, and reports.

instruction: What should I do if OTP is not received during CME login?
output: Retry after a few minutes or contact APMC technical support.

instruction: Can CME login credentials be shared among organization officials?
output: No, the credentials are unique to each organization to maintain data security.

instruction: Who can use the Observer Login?
output: Users designated as observers to monitor CME/CPD programmes without administrative control.

instruction: What access does an observer have?
output: They can view programme details, attendance, and report data but cannot create or edit applications.

instruction: Who can use the APMC Login?
output: Only APMC officials and administrative staff.

instruction: What features are available for APMC staff login?
output: Administrative approval, verification, monitoring of CME/CPD programmes, and report generation.

instruction: Are upcoming CME events available to the general public online?
output: Yes, all approved and upcoming CME programmes can be checked publicly through the portal.

instruction: Will there be notifications sent for upcoming approved CME events?
output: Yes, registered organizations and participants may receive email/SMS alerts for scheduled events.

instruction: Can I download the list of upcoming CME programmes?
output: Yes, it can be viewed or downloaded as a report from the CME portal.

instruction: Is CME registration mandatory for all hospitals?
output: Only organizations wishing to conduct CME/CPD training programmes under APMC approval must register.

instruction: Who issues CME approval in APMC?
output: Only APMC officials and observers are authorized to approve or reject CME applications.

instruction: Can individual doctors register for CME events?
output: Individual doctors can attend approved CME events but cannot register as organizing bodies.

instruction: How long does CME program approval take?
output: Processing time depends on documentation and compliance; provisional approval is usually given within a few working days.

instruction: Is there a fee for organisation registration?
output: Yes, registration and approval fees are applicable based on programme type (as per APMC's fee schedule).

instruction: What happens after successful CME programme approval?
output: Approval details, programme ID, and official confirmation will be displayed on the dashboard and sent via email.

instruction: Can CME program data be edited after submission?
output: No, editing is limited—use Re-Apply option for complete correction if rejection occurs.

instruction: Does APMC monitor CME events after approval?
output: Yes, observers monitor program execution through the observer login portal.

instruction: How do I contact support for CME registration issues?
output: Reach APMC technical team or support number available on the official website during working hours.

instruction: What is the official website to apply for the FMG Internship Program?
output: All eligible Foreign Medical Graduates must use this portal only for internship registration.

instruction: I did not receive an OTP during FMG registration. What should I do?
output: Ensure your mobile number and email are correctly entered and active. Check spam mail. If not received, refresh the page and try again after a few minutes.

instruction: What if I forget my FMG Registration ID or password?
output: Use the “Forgot Password” or “Retrieve Registration ID” option on the login page. Enter your registered email or mobile number to reset your credentials.

instruction: Can I change my mobile number or email after FMG registration?
output: No. The registered mobile number and email ID are linked to your application and cannot be changed once submitted.

instruction: What if I skip a tab or leave it incomplete in FMG application?
output: The application will be considered incomplete and ineligible for processing. Ensure all tabs are properly submitted.

instruction: How do I pay the FMG internship registration fee?
output: After filling the Personal Details tab, proceed to the payment gateway. Choose your payment mode (Visa, Debit/Credit card, Net Banking), agree to the terms, and click “Pay Now.”

instruction: My FMG payment failed, but the amount was deducted. What should I do?
output: Click “Verify and Continue” to refresh the payment status. If the payment is still unsuccessful, a refund will be processed within 7 working days.

instruction: Where can I get my FMG payment receipt?
output: Once your payment is successful, a receipt is generated on-screen and can be downloaded from the ‘Payment History' section.

instruction: What medical qualification information should be submitted for FMG internship?
output: Enter the name of your university and college, course start and end dates, degree issue date, FMGE screening test hall ticket number, intermediate details, and marks obtained in the screening test.

instruction: Is the FMGE Screening Test Pass Certificate mandatory?
output: Yes. Passing the FMGE exam and uploading its certificate is mandatory for internship eligibility.

instruction: How is my region classified for the FMG internship?
output: Region is determined based on education details from classes 6 to 12. Candidates who studied in Andhra Pradesh are classified as Local; others are Non-local. Upload relevant certificates for proof.

instruction: What visa information must be submitted for FMG internship?
output: You need to enter the country of study, admission and completion dates, total course duration, visa entry details, stay duration in India and abroad, and semester details. If an internship was done abroad, details must also be included.

instruction: What file formats are accepted for FMG document uploads?
output: Uploads must be in JPG, JPEG, or PDF format as specified. Photos and signatures must be under 200 KB in size.

instruction: Is embassy attestation mandatory for FMG documents?
output: Yes. All foreign-issued academic documents must be attested or apostilled by the Indian Embassy or relevant authority.

instruction: Is a Police Clearance Certificate necessary for FMG internship?
output: Yes. It is mandatory to upload a recent Police Clearance Certificate (Non-Criminal Record).

instruction: Can I edit my FMG application details after submitting?
output: No, after final submission, the application form cannot be edited. Verify all details carefully before submitting.

instruction: How can I check my FMG application status?
output: Log in to your account and go to the “Application Status” section to check your current progress or verification status.

instruction: Who can I contact for FMG portal technical issues?
output: Use the “Helpdesk” or “Contact Us” link on the portal homepage to access official helpline numbers and support email IDs provided by APMC.

instruction: What is the last date to apply for the FMG internship program?
output: The deadline for registration is announced on the APMC FMG portal homepage. Applications submitted after the deadline will not be accepted.

instruction: Who is eligible to apply for the FMG internship under APMC?
output: Indian citizens who completed their MBBS or equivalent degree from a recognized foreign medical university and have passed the FMGE Screening Test are eligible to apply for the internship.

instruction: What is the process for obtaining registration with APMC?
output: Doctors must submit an application with all required original educational certificates from SSC / equivalent to higher education (UG/PG) uploaded to the APMC server. Final registration certificate issued by APMC must be obtained before applying for No Objection Certificate (NOC) or Good Standing Certificate.

instruction: Can NOC/Good Standing applications be submitted before Final Registration?
output: No, applications for NOC and Good Standing Certificates will be accepted only after the Final Registration Certificate is issued by APMC.

instruction: Can I apply for renewal by post?
output: No, applications for renewal and other services will not be accepted by post or through any third party.

instruction: How will APMC certificates be delivered?
output: All certificates will be sent exclusively through registered post.

instruction: What are the payment methods accepted for APMC services?
output: All UPI, Debit, and Credit card payments are accepted.

instruction: Who needs to obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC)?
output: All Post Graduates who studied or worked in another state must register with the concerned state medical council and obtain an NOC to be registered with APMC.

instruction: Will the NOC be issued as a physical copy?
output: No, the NOC will be sent to the concerned state medical council and the applicant by email only; no physical copy will be issued.

instruction: What happens to the registration number after the NOC is issued?
output: The registration number becomes inactive in the Andhra Pradesh Medical Register immediately after the NOC is issued.

instruction: How can the registration number be reactivated after NOC?
output: Reactivation requires a NOC or Non-Utilization Letter from the concerned state medical council.

instruction: What are the new guidelines for CME credit hours for online CMEs?
output: Two CME credit hours or points will be awarded per day of an 8-hour duration for online CMEs, webinars, or conferences, as per the new NMC guidelines.

instruction: Is slot booking mandatory for CME registrations?
output: Yes, slot booking is mandatory for all CME registrations.

instruction: How early should I attend my CME slot?
output: Attendees should be present at least 20 minutes before the scheduled slot time.

instruction: What is the legal purpose of the Andhra Pradesh Medical Council?
output: To protect, promote, and maintain public health and safety by ensuring doctors meet standards prescribed by the Indian Medical Council's Code of Professional Conduct, Etiquette, and Ethics.

instruction: Can the Medical Council inspect hospitals or institutions?
output: Yes, under Section-20 (iv), the Council can visit or inspect hospitals, nursing homes, or institutions where unethical practices or illegal medical practice activities are suspected.

instruction: What actions can the Council take if standards are not met?
output: The Council will take appropriate action against doctors or institutions not meeting professional standards.

instruction: Who must submit the list of participants for CME/CPD programmes?
output: The organizers must submit the list of participants with details like Council Registration Number, Full Name, Contact Number, Email ID, and Category of the State via Excel format from the Organizer's login.

instruction: How should the CME participant list be submitted?
output: A signed copy of the participant list, with signatures of the Organizing Secretary and the APMC Observer, must be sent in PDF format by email.

instruction: Who is responsible for entering participant details for CME?
output: The Association/Organization organizing the CME must enter details in the provided Excel proforma available in the Organizer's login, coordinating with the APMC Observer.

instruction: Can APMC be held responsible for errors in submitted data?
output: No, APMC will not be responsible for any errors or incomplete data submitted when awarding credit hours.

instruction: How will CME certificates be issued?
output: CME certificates for delegates and faculty members will be issued exclusively via email as E-Certificates.

instruction: Are physical CME certificates requesting signatures accepted?
output: No, physical certificates sent to APMC for signatures will no longer be accepted.

instruction: Who monitors the CME programme?
output: The APMC Observer monitors the entire programme as per the schedule.

instruction: Will CME certificates be issued to all attendees?
output: E-certificates are issued only to delegates who attend the entire programme as per the schedule.

instruction: Can corrections be made after CME delegate details are uploaded?
output: No corrections will be entertained at the APMC level after delegate details are uploaded.

instruction: How does the observer facilitate CME certificate issuance?
output: The observer must provide a digital signature by email to enable issuance of E-Certificates.

instruction: What must organizers do regarding CME programme recording?
output: Organizers must record CME proceedings in audio and video formats and submit them to APMC via pen drive or email.

instruction: When should organizers apply for CME accreditation?
output: Applications for CME accreditation must be submitted at least 30 days before the event; late applications will not be accepted.

instruction: Who can apply for CME accreditation?
output: Only recognized medical associations or medical colleges can apply for CME accreditation; applications from hospitals will not be entertained.

instruction: When must the registration desk close for CME delegates?
output: The delegate registration desk, including spot registration, must close by 11:00 AM on the CME/workshop/conference day.

instruction: How many CME credit hours for attending online CMEs/webinars?
output: You will receive 2 CME credit hours or points per day for attending an 8-hour duration online CME, webinar, or conference as per new NMC guidelines.

instruction: Is slot booking necessary for CME/webinar registrations?
output: Yes, slot booking is mandatory for all CME or webinar registrations.

instruction: When should I join my slot for the CME or webinar?
output: Please ensure your presence at least 20 minutes before your booked slot time.

instruction: What payment methods are accepted for CME/webinar registrations?
output: All UPI, debit card, and credit card payments are accepted.

instruction: How will I receive my CME certificate after attending?
output: All CME certificates will be sent only through registered post.

instruction: How is the NOC sent after issuance?
output: The NOC is sent electronically via email to both the concerned State Medical Council and the candidate. No physical copy is issued.

instruction: What happens to the AP registration number after NOC is issued?
output: The registration number becomes inactive immediately from the Andhra Pradesh Medical Register.

instruction: How do I reactivate my AP registration after obtaining an NOC?
output: You must submit the NOC or Non-Utilization Letter from the concerned State Medical Council to reactivate your registration.

instruction: What documents are needed to apply for NOC from APMC?
output: Typical documents include the filled application form, registration certificate (final or renewal), proof of the purpose of NOC letter, PG qualification certificate (if applicable), and passport-sized photographs.

instruction: Is a physical registration certificate issued after NOC?
output: No, the process is online and the NOC is sent via email; physical copies are not issued.

instruction: What if I do not have a final registration or renewal certificate for NOC?
output: You need to attach the relevant registration or renewal certificate, along with a letter explaining the purpose for NOC from the respective Medical Council.

instruction: Are there specific fees for obtaining NOC?
output: Yes, the fee varies depending on the type of applicant but generally around 5,000 rupees, as per the last available update.

instruction: How long does it take to process an NOC application?
output: Processing times can vary; typically, the applicant should check with the APMC office or the online portal for current timelines.

instruction: What is the main objective of the APMC?
output: The primary objective is to protect, promote, and maintain public health and safety by ensuring that doctors meet the standards prescribed by the Indian Medical Council's Code of Professional Conduct, Etiquette and Ethics Regulations, 2002, as amended.

instruction: What kind of standards does APMC ensure in medical practice?
output: APMC makes sure doctors follow ethical, professional, and conduct standards defined by the Indian Medical Council, promoting good medical practice.

instruction: What actions can the APMC take if standards are not met?
output: The Council takes appropriate action, including inquiries and disciplinary measures, against doctors not meeting required standards.

instruction: Does APMC have powers to inspect hospitals or institutions?
output: Yes. Under Section-20(iv), APMC can visit or inspect any hospital, nursing home, institution, or place where unethical medical practices occur, especially where unqualified persons or doctors from other systems practice modern scientific medicine.

instruction: Who can constitute committees for inspections under APMC?
output: The Chairman of the Council can form two or three-member committees from council members (excluding ex-officio members) to conduct inspections.

instruction: Is APMC responsible for registering medical practitioners?
output: Yes, it registers eligible medical graduates and maintains the medical register of practitioners.

instruction: How does APMC operate organizationally?
output: APMC has an Executive Committee and several sub-committees such as Finance, Ethics, and CME Committees to manage its functions.

instruction: When was the APMC established?
output: It was established by Act No.23 of 1968 and formally constituted through a government notification in December 1991.

instruction: How does APMC protect public health?
output: By regulating professional standards, inspecting healthcare facilities, disciplining unethical practices, and ensuring doctors follow updated medical laws and ethics.

output:
Contact number - 0866 245 5280, 0866 245 5281 
 WhatsApp number - 90309 99616

instruction: What is the Mail or gmail or email of apmc to contact?

 contact@apmedicalcouncil.in

instruction: Is there a mobile app for accessing APMC services?
output: No, APMC doesn't have mobile app for accessing its services.

instruction: Who are the executive members of APMC?

instruction: Registration for the FMGs Internship Program
output: 1. Click 'Registration' and fill out the form with your name, mobile number, email ID, date of birth, and create a password. 2. Enter the captcha and click 'Generate OTP.' 3. Enter the OTP you receive on your mobile number and email. 4. After successful submission, a Registration ID will be displayed and also sent to you via SMS and email. 5. Ensure your mobile number remains active throughout the registration process.

instruction: FMG Candidate Login/Sign-in
output: 1. Go to the Login page. 2. Enter your User ID (which is your Registration ID), password, and the captcha. 3. Click 'Login' to access the application submission portal.

instruction: Steps to Submit the FMG Application
output: 1. After logging in, select 'Services' from the menu bar, then choose 'Application Submission.' 2. The submission screen is organized into several tabs: Personal Details, Fee Payment, Medical Qualification Details, Local/Non-local Region, Visa Details, and Upload Documents. 3. You must fill in each tab completely. Failing to complete any tab or submit the application will result in ineligibility.

instruction: Required Personal Details for FMG Internship Registration
output: You will need to provide the following personal details: Candidate name, Father's and mother's name, Gender, Blood group, Email, Residential address, Nationality, Passport number, Date of Birth, Aadhaar number, Mobile number, and Permanent address details.

instruction: Fee Payment Process for FMG Internship Registration
output: 1. After filling in your Personal Details, you will be directed to the payment gateway. 2. Select your preferred payment mode (e.g., Visa, Debit/Credit card, Net Banking). 3. Agree to the terms and conditions and click 'Pay Now.' 4. If a transaction fails but the amount is deducted, click 'Verify and Continue' to update the status. Refunds are processed within 7 working days. 5. Upon successful payment, a receipt will be displayed, and you can proceed to the next steps.

instruction: Medical Qualification Details for FMG Internship Registration
output: You must submit the following medical qualification data: University and college names, Degree issue date, Course start and end dates, Intermediate equivalent details, Screening test hall ticket number, and Marks secured.

instruction: Region Classification (Local vs Non-local) for FMG Internship Registration
output: Based on your education details from classes 6 to 12, you will be classified as either Local (Andhra Pradesh region) or Non-local (other states). You are required to upload the corresponding certificates for verification based on your classification.

instruction: Required Visa Information for FMG Internship Registration
output: You must provide the following visa information: Country of study, Date of admission, Course completion date, Total course duration, Internship details if completed abroad, Visa entry form details (including place of arrival/departure), Duration of stay in India and abroad, and Semester details.

instruction: Required Documents for FMG Internship Application Submission
output: You must upload the following documents: Candidate photo and signature (JPG/JPEG, max 200 KB), Eligibility Certificate from MCI/NMC, Admission Letter, Provisional or Original Degree Certificate, Embassy Certification/Apostille, Academic Transcript, FMGE Screening Test Pass Certificate, SSC/Equivalent for proof of birth date, 10+2/Intermediate Verification Letter, Scanned Copy of Passport (all pages), Affidavit on Rs. 20/- non-judicial stamp paper, Aadhaar Card, Credential/Notarial Certificate, and Police Clearance Certificate.

instruction: Guidelines for the APMC FMG Internship Registration Process
output: Key guidelines to follow are: • The mobile number used for registration must be kept active until the process is complete. • All tabs in the application must be filled out completely. • If a payment fails, use the 'Verify and Continue' option; refunds are processed within 7 working days. • All uploaded documents must strictly comply with the specified size and format instructions.

instruction: Procedure To check Slot Availability
output: Procedure for ONLINE SLOT AVAILABILITY: 1. Select the date 2. Select the slot type (Tatkal or Non Tatkal) 3. Click on Check availability

instruction: Procedure for Slot Booking
output: Procedure for SLOT BOOKING:  1. Select Date  2. Select Slot Type (Tatkal or Non Tatkal) 3. Select Slot Period  4. Select Slot Time at the Doctor Profile:  1. Enter Registration Number  2. Enter Doctor Name  3. Enter Mobile Number  4. Click on verify your Mobile Number

instruction: Andhra Pradesh Medical Council (APMC) - Application & Annexures
output: APMC: Application & Annexures:

output: Contact & Service Information: 1. Address:  2nd Floor, Dr. N.T.R. University of Health Sciences, Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh - 520008 2. Contact:  Phone: 0866 245 5280 / 0866 245 5281 , WhatsApp: 90309 99616 3. Counter Timings:  10:00 AM - 04:30 PM 4. Lunch Break: 01:30 PM - 02:00 PM 5. Working Days:  Monday - Friday 6. Service Turnaround Time (TAT):  • TATKAL: 4 Working Days per service  • NON-TATKAL: 3 Weeks per service

instruction: Regular fee for Provisional Registration (IMG)
output: What is the regular fee for Provisional Registration (Indian Medical Graduates - IMG)? ₹1000 (within one year after MBBS completion).

instruction: Late fee for Provisional Registration (IMG)
output: What is the late fee for Provisional Registration (IMG)? ₹5000 if delayed by one year or more after the date of MBBS degree issuance.

instruction: Regular fee for Provisional Registration (FMG AP)
output: What is the regular fee for Provisional Registration (Foreign Medical Graduates - FMG) who studied 10+2 in Andhra Pradesh? ₹2000.

instruction: Late fee for Provisional Registration (FMG AP)
output: Is there any late fee for FMG (AP)? No, there is no late fee.

instruction: Regular fee for Provisional Registration (FMG Other States)
output: What is the regular fee for Provisional Registration (FMG) who studied 10+2 in other states? ₹3000.

instruction: Late fee for Provisional Registration (FMG Other States)
output: Is there any late fee for FMG (Other States)? No, there is no late fee.

instruction: Regular fee for Final Registration (IMG)
output: What is the regular fee for Final Registration (IMG)? ₹4000 (within one year after internship completion).

instruction: Late fee for Final Registration (IMG)
output: What is the late fee for Final Registration (IMG)? ₹5000 if delayed by one year or more after internship completion.

instruction: Regular fee for Final Registration (FMG AP)
output: What is the regular fee for Final Registration (FMG) with 10+2 in Andhra Pradesh? ₹6000.

instruction: Regular fee for Final Registration (FMG Other States)
output: What is the regular fee for Final Registration (FMG) with 10+2 in other states? ₹8000.

instruction: Late fee for Final Registration (FMG)
output: Is there any late fee for Final Registration (FMG)? No, there is no late fee.

instruction: Fees for Additional Qualification Registration
output: What are the fees for Additional Qualification Registration • Diploma: ₹3000 • Postgraduate (PG): ₹6000 • Super Specialty: ₹10000

instruction: Late fee for Additional Qualification Registration
output: What is the late fee for Additional Qualification Registration? ₹5000 if delayed by one year or more after the date of degree issuance.

instruction: Renewal fee for MBBS Final Registration
output: What is the renewal fee for MBBS Final Registration? ₹4000 (valid for 5 years; must be renewed every 5 years).

instruction: Late fee for Renewal of Registration
output: What is the late fee for Renewal of Registration? ₹1000 if delayed up to one year after expiry.

instruction: Policy on Renewal Delay Beyond One Year
output: What happens if the renewal is delayed beyond one year? Renewal is not permitted; reregistration is required.

instruction: Fee for Re-Registration
output: What is the fee for Re-Registration? ₹8000 (during the 2nd year after expiry).

instruction: Fee for Re-Registration Delay Beyond 2nd Year
output: What if the delay extends beyond the 2nd year? ₹14000 irrespective of the additional delay.

instruction: Fee for Good Standing or Certificate Verification
output: What is the fee for APMC Good Standing or Certificate Verification? ₹5000 (no late fee).

instruction: Fee for Name Change or Certificate Correction
output: What is the fee for Change of Name or Certificate Correction? ₹500 (no late fee).

instruction: Fee for NOC and Certificate Verification
output: What is the fee for NOC, Registration Certificate Verification, or NMC Good Standing Certificate? ₹2000 (no late fee).

instruction: Registration Status Following NOC Issue
output: What happens once an NOC is issued? The registration becomes inactive.

instruction: Fee for Issuing Duplicate Certificate
output: What is the fee for issuing any Duplicate Certificate? The same as the original registration fee (no late fee).

instruction: CME Accreditation Fees
output: What are the CME Accreditation fees? One-day Programme: ₹5000 Two-day Programme: ₹10000 Three-day Programme / State Conference: ₹15000 National Conference: ₹20000 (No late fee applies).

instruction: Fee for Reactivation of Registration
output: What is the fee for Reactivation of Registration? ₹1000 (no late fee). Note: Requires NOC or Non-utilization letter from the concerned State Medical Council.

instruction: Fee for Non-Utilization Letter
output: What is the fee for Non-Utilization Letter to other State Medical Councils? ₹1000 (no late fee).

instruction: Fee for Provisional Registration Revalidation
output: What is the fee for Provisional Registration Revalidation? ₹2000 (no late fee). Note: Revalidation is allowed within 2 years as per CRMI Regulations 2021.

instruction: Fee for Identity Card
output: What is the fee for an Identity Card? ₹500 (if required, no late fee).

instruction: Tatkal Fee for Registration Services
output: What is the Tatkal (urgent) fee for all registration services? ₹2000 extra for each registration service.

instruction: Renewal Fee for Doctors Practicing Outside Andhra Pradesh
output: What is the renewal fee for doctors registered with APMC but practicing in other states or abroad? ₹6000 with a valid NOC (issued within the last 3 months) and proof of other state registration or visa/passport copy.

instruction: Delay Fee for Late Collection of Original Certificate
output: What is the additional delay fee for collecting the original registration certificate late? ₹500 if collected after 3 months from the registration date.

instruction: Delay Fee for Pending CME Submission
output: What is the delay fee for submitting pending CMEs after renewal or re-registration? ₹500 if delayed beyond 3 months.

instruction: Procedure for medical registration search/Doctor registration search
output: You can search by Doctor Name or Registration Number: • Enter your Name • Enter your Gender • Enter your Father Name • Click on Search By Name OR • Enter Final Medical Registration No. • Click on Search By Registration No.

instruction: Organisation Registration For CME/CPD Programmes
output: To register your organisation for CME/CPD Programmes, please visit the official registration website.

instruction: Rejection of CME/CPD Program Application
output: Rejection reasons for a CME/CPD program application may include: • Incomplete Documentation • Late Submission • Incorrect Information • Failure to adhere to APMC guidelines or submission protocols.

instruction: Deadline for Re-submission of CME Application
output: While APMC does not specify an official deadline, it is advisable to re-submit the corrected application at the earliest to avoid further delays or expiration of program dates.

instruction: Procedure for an Organization to Register on the AP Medical Council CME/CPD Portal
output: To register an organization on the AP Medical Council CME/CPD application: • Click the Organization Registration tab at the top left corner. • Complete all mandatory fields: Organization Type, Contact Number, Organization Name, Email, Organisation Registration Number, and Address. • Upload the Organization Registration Certificate (PDF, under 400kb). • Click Save Details. An acknowledgment ID will be generated, and provisional approval will be sent.

instruction: How to Re-apply for CME Registration After Rejection
output: If your CME registration application is rejected: • Use the 'Re-Apply for Org. Registration' option on the website. • Resubmit the required information and documents through the provided link.

instruction: CME Organizations Login Procedure
output: To log in as a CME Organization: • Click the Organisation Login button at the top right corner of the home page. • Enter your User ID (Acknowledgement ID). • Click Sign In. • Enter the OTP sent to your registered mobile number to access your dashboard.

instruction: Observer Login Procedure
output: Steps for Observer Login: • Click the Observer option available on the login page. • Enter observer credentials. • Access the system in observer mode. This is for users who need to observe or monitor CME/CPD programmes without full administrative access.

instruction: APMC Staff Login
output: This login is available for AP Medical Council officials and administrative staff. If you are a staff member, you can select your designation, enter your user ID, and log in.

instruction: Finding Upcoming CME Programmes/Events
output: To check for upcoming CME Programmes and Events, visit the official programmes page on the APMC website.
